methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Standard Electron operates in the Climate & Energy sector at the stealth stage. The valuation universe comprises nine sector peers: one public company (LNZA) and eight private entities including Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen. This represents a fragmented landscape with limited direct comparables at identical development stages.

Peer-median analysis proved inapplicable due to insufficient clustering. Within the nine-peer Climate & Energy universe, no subset of three or more companies shared both the target's stage and valuation tier, precluding statistically meaningful median derivation. Same-stage and adjacent-stage filters each yielded fewer than three datapoints. An all-sector median would introduce excessive noise given the heterogeneity in business models, capital intensity, and market maturity across the peer set.

Given data constraints, triangulation methods could not be reliably executed. Valuation support would require either (1) expansion of the comp universe via relaxed stage or sector filters—introducing methodological compromise—or (2) application of forward-looking methods (DCF, scenario analysis) keyed to the target's specific unit economics and deployment timeline, for which no data was provided. The absence of a viable narrow comp set and lack of financial detail on Standard Electron itself represent the primary valuation uncertainties. Proceeding would require either acquisition of additional peer financials or explicit reliance on non-comparable approaches.
